rationale_for_baseline_tier: Charcot's triad (fever with rigors, RUQ pain, jaundice) with systemic sepsis physiology (hypotension, tachycardia, tachypnoea) in a patient with chronic biliary disease is acute ascending cholangitis with severe biliary sepsis — an immediate threat to life. Paracetamol has failed and the patient is toxic and deteriorating. The patient/spouse can themselves report time-critical emergency features (high fever + rigors + right-upper-quadrant pain + jaundice, feeling faint/toxic), which clears the telehealth T3 ceiling: T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate tier for definitive IV antibiotics, fluids and urgent biliary decompression.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New confusion, drowsiness or altered consciousness (Reynolds' pentad)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — stay on the line with 000
escalation_edges.rationale: Confusion plus hypotension signals septic shock / Reynolds' pentad — a further deterioration demanding emergency resuscitation and urgent biliary drainage.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he becomes confused, very drowsy or hard to wake, tell 000 immediately — this means the infection is affecting his whole body.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Collapse or stops breathing before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.rationale: Cardiorespiratory arrest from overwhelming sepsis — CPR and paramedic resuscitation are the critical resource.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he collapses or stops breathing, tell 000 straight away and start CPR if you know how — keep him flat.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening fever, cold clammy skin, or feeling faint while waiting
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Keep him lying flat and stay on the line with 000 until paramedics arrive
escalation_edges.rationale: Rising sepsis / falling blood pressure before transport; lying flat supports perfusion and paramedics can give fluids en route.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the fever gets worse, his skin goes cold and clammy, or he feels faint, keep him lying flat and let 000 know — help is on the way.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Chronic stricturing biliary disease (suspected PSC) with a prior cholangitis episode 6 months ago and known ulcerative colitis
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Recurrent biliary infection is likely and biliary decompression may be needed — reinforces that this is a hospital emergency, not a home or GP problem, and that the underlying disease needs specialist surveillance afterwards.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse is present and made the call; patient is bedbound and cannot mobilise alone
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Direct clear instructions to the spouse — call 000, keep him flat and nil-by-mouth, unlock the door for paramedics, and watch for confusion or collapse.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an emergency ambulance right now and say he has a high fever with violent shaking, right-sided tummy pain and yellow eyes. Keep him lying flat and resting. Do not give him anything to eat or drink. Do not drive him yourselves — wait for the ambulance. Stay with him, unlock the front door for the paramedics, and watch for any confusion, drowsiness or collapse.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or a GP appointment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary; arrange hepatology/gastroenterology follow-up for MRCP, management of the underlying biliary (PSC) disease, and cholangiocarcinoma surveillance given the weight loss and obstructive jaundice.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating this as a UC flare, a routine GP problem or self-care with antipyretics instead of an emergency.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Delayed IV antibiotics and biliary drainage allow septic shock and multi-organ failure — a high-mortality outcome.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising urgent same-day GP or in-person review rather than emergency ambulance for a patient with sepsis physiology.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Not over-triage here — T5 is correct; listed only for completeness.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact cause and level of biliary obstruction, sepsis severity and whether cholangiocarcinoma is present cannot be determined remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate on the recognisable emergency pattern (Charcot's triad + toxic/septic picture) without waiting for confirmation; let the hospital confirm with bloods, imaging and MRCP.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Delaying escalation to gather more data, trialling oral antibiotics or paracetamol, or reassuring on the basis of the known UC diagnosis.
